Settings

we need to make a website for a small nonprofit cancer research and teaching institute. Can you make it for us?

Yes—I can help you plan and actually write the content and code for the site.

To get started, I’ll outline a simple, professional site structure for a small nonprofit cancer research and teaching institute, then provide example text and starter code you can use or give to a developer.

1. Basic site structure

A clean, credible nonprofit site can work well with 5–7 core pages:

  1. Home
  2. About
  3. Research
  4. Education & Training
  5. Get Involved / Support Us
  6. News & Events (optional but recommended)
  7. Contact

We can do this as a single-page scrolling site or as separate pages. I’ll show a multi-section single page (easier to host and maintain), and you can later split into multiple pages if needed.

2. Branding questions (so I can tailor it better)

If you answer these, I can customize everything:

  1. Institute name and location:
  2. Short tagline (e.g., “Advancing Cancer Research. Training the Next Generation.”):
  3. Primary mission in 1–2 sentences:
  4. Main audience: (patients, researchers, donors, students, clinicians, or a mix?)
  5. Do you already have a logo or color preferences? (e.g., blue/white, green, etc.)
  6. Do you want online donations (e.g., via PayPal/Stripe/Network for Good) right away?
  7. Any specific cancer focus? (e.g., breast, pediatric, hematologic, etc.)

I’ll use neutral placeholders for now; you can replace them.

3. Example site text (you can edit names/details)

Home (Hero section)

[Institute Name]
Advancing Cancer Research. Empowering Learners. Improving Lives.

[Institute Name] is a nonprofit cancer research and teaching institute dedicated to discovering better treatments, training the next generation of clinicians and scientists, and sharing knowledge with our community.

  • Independent, nonprofit institute
  • Collaborative research across disciplines
  • Education for students, clinicians, and the public

[Button: Learn More] [Button: Support Our Work]

About

Our Mission

[Institute Name] advances cancer research and education to reduce the burden of cancer for patients, families, and communities. We integrate laboratory and clinical research with hands-on teaching to move discoveries from bench to bedside.

Our Vision

A world where cancer is preventable, detectable early, and treated with therapies tailored to each individual.

Our Values

  • Scientific excellence – Rigorous, transparent, and reproducible research
  • Compassion – Centering patients and families in everything we do
  • Collaboration – Working across disciplines and institutions
  • Education – Training tomorrow’s cancer researchers and clinicians
  • Equity – Expanding access to knowledge, care, and careers in science

Leadership

  • [Dr. First Last], Founder & Director – Brief 1–2 sentence bio.
  • [Name], Director of Research – 1–2 sentence bio.
  • [Name], Director of Education – 1–2 sentence bio.

(You can list your board of directors, scientific advisory board, and partner institutions here.)

Research

Our Research Areas

We focus on research that can make a meaningful difference for patients:

  • Translational Oncology – Connecting laboratory discoveries to clinical trials.
  • Cancer Biology – Understanding how cancers start, grow, and spread.
  • Early Detection & Screening – Identifying cancer earlier, when it’s most treatable.
  • Health Disparities in Cancer – Reducing gaps in outcomes across communities.

(Adjust these to match your real programs.)

Our Approach

  • Collaboration: We partner with universities, hospitals, and community clinics.
  • Open Science: We share data, methods, and findings whenever possible.
  • Mentored Research: Students and trainees contribute to active research projects.

Selected Projects

  • Project Title 1 – 1–2 sentences describing the goal and impact.
  • Project Title 2 – 1–2 sentences describing the goal and impact.

[Button: View Our Publications] (optional link to PubMed / Google Scholar profile)

Education & Training

Training the Next Generation

We offer education and training programs for:

  • Undergraduate and graduate students
  • Residents and fellows
  • Practicing clinicians and allied health professionals
  • Community members and patient advocates

Programs

  • Research Internships – Supervised research experiences for students.
  • Workshops & Short Courses – Focused training in cancer biology, methods, or clinical topics.
  • Seminar Series – Talks by invited researchers and clinicians.
  • Community Education – Public lectures on prevention, screening, and survivorship.

[Button: View Current Opportunities] (link to a simple list or PDF)

Get Involved / Support Us

Support Our Work

As a nonprofit institute, we rely on grants and donations to sustain and expand our research and teaching.

Your support helps us:

  • Launch high-risk, high-reward research projects
  • Provide scholarships for trainees
  • Offer free or low-cost community education programs
  • Invest in critical laboratory equipment and technology

[Button: Donate]
(This can link to PayPal, Stripe, or another donation platform.)

Other Ways to Help

  • Partner with us – Collaborate on research or educational initiatives.
  • Volunteer – Help with events, outreach, and administrative support.
  • Spread the word – Share our work with your network.

[Button: Contact Us about Partnerships]

News & Events

(You can keep this simple at first.)

Latest News

  • [Date] – Headline – 1-sentence summary and link to a full story or PDF.
  • [Date] – Headline – 1-sentence summary.

Upcoming Events

  • [Event Name] – [Date] – Short description, target audience, and registration info.

[Button: Join Our Mailing List]

Contact

[Institute Name]

Address: [Street, City, State, Country]
Phone: [Phone Number]
Email: [info@yourdomain.org]

[Optional: Simple contact form with Name, Email, Message]

Office hours, parking, and accessibility information can also go here.

4. Starter HTML/CSS template

This is a simple, responsive one-page site you can host anywhere (GitHub Pages, Netlify, your own server). Copy this into an index.html file and open in a browser to see it.

You’ll want to replace all [Institute Name], text, emails, etc., with your own details.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <title>[Institute Name] – Cancer Research & Education</title>
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <meta name="description" content="[Institute Name] is a nonprofit cancer research and teaching institute dedicated to advancing cancer science and training the next generation of clinicians and researchers.">

  <style>
    :root {
      --primary: #1e4e79;
      --primary-light: #2f74b5;
      --accent: #ef6c00;
      --bg: #f7f9fc;
      --text: #222;
      --muted: #666;
      --max-width: 1100px;
      --radius: 6px;
    }

    * {
      box-sizing: border-box;
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
    }

    body {
      font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif;
      color: var(--text);
      background: white;
      line-height: 1.6;
    }

    a {
      color: var(--primary);
      text-decoration: none;
    }

    a:hover {
      text-decoration: underline;
    }

    header {
      background: white;
      border-bottom: 1px solid #dde3ec;
      position: sticky;
      top: 0;
      z-index: 100;
    }

    .nav {
      max-width: var(--max-width);
      margin: 0 auto;
      padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: space-between;
    }

    .logo {
      font-weight: 700;
      color: var(--primary);
      font-size: 1.1rem;
    }

    .logo span {
      font-weight: 400;
      color: var(--muted);
      font-size: 0.9rem;
    }

    nav ul {
      list-style: none;
      display: flex;
      gap: 1.25rem;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
    }

    nav a {
      font-weight: 500;
    }

    .hero {
      background: radial-gradient(circle at top left, #e3f2fd, #ffffff);
      border-bottom: 1px solid #dde3ec;
    }

    .hero-inner {
      max-width: var(--max-width);
      margin: 0 auto;
      padding: 3rem 1rem 3.5rem;
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1.5fr) minmax(0, 1.2fr);
      gap: 2rem;
      align-items: center;
    }

    .hero h1 {
      font-size: clamp(2rem, 3vw + 1rem, 3rem);
      margin-bottom: 1rem;
      color: #123456;
    }

    .hero p {
      margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
      color: var(--muted);
      font-size: 1.02rem;
    }

    .hero-highlights {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 0.75rem 1.5rem;
      margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      color: var(--muted);
    }

    .button-row {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 0.75rem;
    }

    .btn {
      display: inline-block;
      padding: 0.65rem 1.25rem;
      border-radius: 999px;
      border: 1px solid transparent;
      font-weight: 600;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      cursor: pointer;
      text-align: center;
    }

    .btn-primary {
      background: var(--primary);
      color: white;
      border-color: var(--primary);
    }

    .btn-primary:hover {
      background: var(--primary-light);
      text-decoration: none;
    }

    .btn-outline {
      background: white;
      color: var(--primary);
      border-color: var(--primary);
    }

    .btn-outline:hover {
      background: #e3f2fd;
      text-decoration: none;
    }

    .hero-panel {
      background: white;
      border-radius: var(--radius);
      padding: 1.5rem;
      box-shadow: 0 12px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
      border: 1px solid #e3e8f0;
    }

    .hero-panel h2 {
      font-size: 1.1rem;
      margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
    }

    .hero-panel ul {
      list-style: none;
      font-size: 0.92rem;
      color: var(--muted);
    }

    .hero-panel li {
      margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
      padding-left: 1.1rem;
      position: relative;
    }

    .hero-panel li::before {
      content: "•";
      position: absolute;
      left: 0;
      color: var(--primary);
    }

    main {
      padding: 2.5rem 0 3rem;
    }

    section {
      padding: 2.5rem 1rem;
    }

    .section-inner {
      max-width: var(--max-width);
      margin: 0 auto;
    }

    h2.section-title {
      font-size: 1.6rem;
      margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
      color: #123456;
    }

    .two-column {
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1.3fr) minmax(0, 1fr);
      gap: 2rem;
      align-items: flex-start;
    }

    .card-grid {
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(230px, 1fr));
      gap: 1.25rem;
      margin-top: 1rem;
    }

    .card {
      background: white;
      border-radius: var(--radius);
      padding: 1.25rem;
      border: 1px solid #e3e8f0;
    }

    .card h3 {
      font-size: 1.05rem;
      margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
    }

    .muted {
      color: var(--muted);
      font-size: 0.95rem;
    }

    .list {
      list-style: disc;
      padding-left: 1.3rem;
    }

    .list li + li {
      margin-top: 0.35rem;
    }

    .pill {
      display: inline-block;
      padding: 0.25rem 0.7rem;
      border-radius: 999px;
      background: #e3f2fd;
      color: var(--primary);
      font-size: 0.8rem;
      font-weight: 600;
      letter-spacing: 0.03em;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
    }

    .highlight-box {
      border-left: 3px solid var(--primary);
      padding-left: 0.9rem;
      margin: 0.75rem 0 0.25rem;
    }

    .split {
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(260px, 1fr));
      gap: 1.5rem;
      margin-top: 0.5rem;
    }

    .badge-list {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 0.4rem;
      margin-top: 0.5rem;
    }

    .badge {
      padding: 0.2rem 0.6rem;
      border-radius: 999px;
      border: 1px solid #c5d1e0;
      font-size: 0.8rem;
      color: var(--muted);
      background: #f5f7fb;
    }

    footer {
      border-top: 1px solid #dde3ec;
      background: #f6f8fc;
      padding: 1.5rem 1rem;
      font-size: 0.9rem;
      color: var(--muted);
    }

    .footer-inner {
      max-width: var(--max-width);
      margin: 0 auto;
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: space-between;
      gap: 0.75rem;
    }

    .footer-links {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 0.75rem;
    }

    /* Responsive */
    @media (max-width: 850px) {
      .hero-inner,
      .two-column {
        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
      }

      nav ul {
        display: none; /* For simplicity – convert to mobile menu later if needed */
      }

      .hero-inner {
        padding-top: 2.25rem;
      }

      header {
        position: static;
      }
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>

<header>
  <div class="nav">
    <div class="logo">
      [Institute Name] <span>Cancer Research & Education</span>
    </div>
    <nav aria-label="Main navigation">
      <ul>
        <li><a href="#about">About</a></li>
        <li><a href="#research">Research</a></li>
        <li><a href="#education">Education</a></li>
        <li><a href="#support">Support</a></li>
        <li><a href="#news">News & Events</a></li>
        <li><a href="#contact">Contact</a></li>
      </ul>
    </nav>
  </div>
</header>

<section class="hero" id="top">
  <div class="hero-inner">
    <div>
      <h1>Advancing Cancer Research. Empowering Learners. Improving Lives.</h1>
      <p>
        [Institute Name] is a nonprofit cancer research and teaching institute
        dedicated to discovering better treatments, training the next generation
        of clinicians and scientists, and sharing knowledge with our community.
      </p>
      <div class="hero-highlights">
        <span>• Independent nonprofit</span>
        <span>• Translational cancer research</span>
        <span>• Education & training programs</span>
      </div>
      <div class="button-row">
        <a href="#about" class="btn btn-primary">Learn More</a>
        <a href="#support" class="btn btn-outline">Support Our Work</a>
      </div>
    </div>
    <aside class="hero-panel" aria-label="Key priorities">
      <h2>Our Focus Areas</h2>
      <ul>
        <li>Translational oncology – from bench to bedside</li>
        <li>Early detection and screening strategies</li>
        <li>Health equity and cancer disparities</li>
        <li>Education for students, clinicians, and communities</li>
      </ul>
    </aside>
  </div>
</section>

<main>
  <!-- About -->
  <section id="about">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">About Us</h2>
      <div class="two-column">
        <div>
          <span class="pill">Our Mission</span>
          <p>
            [Institute Name] advances cancer research and education to reduce the
            burden of cancer for patients, families, and communities. We integrate
            laboratory and clinical research with hands-on teaching to move
            discoveries from bench to bedside.
          </p>
          <div class="highlight-box">
            <strong>Vision:</strong> A world where cancer is preventable,
            detectable early, and treated with therapies tailored to each
            individual.
          </div>
          <h3 style="margin-top:1.25rem;">Our Values</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li><strong>Scientific excellence</strong> – Rigorous, transparent, and reproducible research.</li>
            <li><strong>Compassion</strong> – Centering patients and families in everything we do.</li>
            <li><strong>Collaboration</strong> – Working across disciplines and institutions.</li>
            <li><strong>Education</strong> – Training tomorrow’s cancer researchers and clinicians.</li>
            <li><strong>Equity</strong> – Expanding access to knowledge, care, and careers in science.</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Leadership</h3>
          <p class="muted">
            Our work is guided by a multidisciplinary team of scientists,
            clinicians, and educators.
          </p>
          <div class="card" style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            <h3>Executive Team</h3>
            <p class="muted">
              <strong>Dr. First Last</strong>, Founder & Director – 1–2 sentence
              bio highlighting expertise and vision.
            </p>
            <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.4rem;">
              <strong>Name</strong>, Director of Research – 1–2 sentence bio.
            </p>
            <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.4rem;">
              <strong>Name</strong>, Director of Education – 1–2 sentence bio.
            </p>
          </div>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.6rem;">
            For a full list of our board of directors and advisors, please
            <a href="#contact">contact us</a>.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Research -->
  <section id="research" style="background: var(--bg);">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">Research</h2>
      <div class="two-column">
        <div>
          <span class="pill">Scientific Programs</span>
          <p>
            We focus on research that has the potential to make a meaningful
            difference for patients and communities, from fundamental cancer
            biology to early detection and survivorship.
          </p>
          <div class="card-grid">
            <div class="card">
              <h3>Translational Oncology</h3>
              <p class="muted">
                Connecting discoveries in the laboratory with early-phase
                clinical studies to move promising therapies toward patients.
              </p>
            </div>
            <div class="card">
              <h3>Cancer Biology</h3>
              <p class="muted">
                Investigating how cancers start, grow, and spread to uncover
                new targets for prevention and treatment.
              </p>
            </div>
            <div class="card">
              <h3>Early Detection & Screening</h3>
              <p class="muted">
                Developing approaches to detect cancer earlier, when it is
                most treatable.
              </p>
            </div>
            <div class="card">
              <h3>Health Disparities</h3>
              <p class="muted">
                Studying and addressing inequities in cancer risk, care, and
                outcomes across populations.
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Selected Projects</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li><strong>Project Title 1</strong> – Short description of goals and potential impact.</li>
            <li><strong>Project Title 2</strong> – Short description of goals and potential impact.</li>
            <li><strong>Project Title 3</strong> – Short description of goals and potential impact.</li>
          </ul>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            For a list of our publications, please see our
            <a href="#">PubMed/Google Scholar profile</a> or
            <a href="#contact">contact us</a> for more information.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Education -->
  <section id="education">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">Education & Training</h2>
      <div class="split">
        <div>
          <span class="pill">Training Programs</span>
          <p>
            We are committed to training the next generation of cancer
            researchers, clinicians, and informed community leaders.
          </p>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li><strong>Research Internships</strong> for undergraduate and graduate students.</li>
            <li><strong>Workshops & Short Courses</strong> in cancer biology, methods, and clinical topics.</li>
            <li><strong>Seminar Series</strong> with invited experts from partner institutions.</li>
            <li><strong>Community Education</strong> on cancer prevention, screening, and survivorship.</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Who We Serve</h3>
          <p class="muted">
            Our educational offerings reach learners at many stages:
          </p>
          <div class="badge-list">
            <span class="badge">Students</span>
            <span class="badge">Residents & Fellows</span>
            <span class="badge">Clinicians</span>
            <span class="badge">Researchers</span>
            <span class="badge">Community Members</span>
          </div>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            For current opportunities and application details, please
            <a href="#contact">contact our education office</a>.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Support -->
  <section id="support" style="background: var(--bg);">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">Support Our Work</h2>
      <div class="two-column">
        <div>
          <span class="pill">Nonprofit Impact</span>
          <p>
            As a nonprofit institute, we rely on grants and philanthropic
            support to sustain and expand our research and educational
            programs.
          </p>
          <p class="muted">
            Your gift helps us:
          </p>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li>Launch high-risk, high-reward research projects.</li>
            <li>Provide scholarships and stipends for trainees.</li>
            <li>Offer free or low-cost community education programs.</li>
            <li>Invest in critical laboratory equipment and technology.</li>
          </ul>
          <div class="button-row" style="margin-top:1rem;">
            <a href="#contact" class="btn btn-primary">Discuss Giving</a>
            <a href="#" class="btn btn-outline">Donate Online</a>
          </div>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.5rem;font-size:0.85rem;">
            [Institute Name] is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.
            Contributions may be tax-deductible as allowed by law.
          </p>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Other Ways to Get Involved</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li><strong>Partner with us</strong> on research or educational initiatives.</li>
            <li><strong>Volunteer</strong> to support events and outreach.</li>
            <li><strong>Host an event</strong> to raise awareness and funds for our work.</li>
            <li><strong>Stay informed</strong> by joining our mailing list.</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- News & Events -->
  <section id="news">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">News & Events</h2>
      <div class="split">
        <div>
          <h3>Latest News</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li><strong>[Date]</strong> – Headline about a recent publication, grant, or award.</li>
            <li><strong>[Date]</strong> – Headline about a new program or partnership.</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Upcoming Events</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li><strong>[Event Name]</strong> – [Date]. Brief description and audience.</li>
            <li><strong>[Seminar/Workshop]</strong> – [Date]. Brief description.</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Contact -->
  <section id="contact" style="background: var(--bg);">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">Contact</h2>
      <div class="split">
        <div>
          <p>
            We welcome inquiries from prospective collaborators, students,
            donors, and members of the public.
          </p>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            <strong>Address:</strong><br />
            [Street Address]<br />
            [City, State, ZIP]<br />
            [Country]
          </p>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.5rem;">
            <strong>Phone:</strong> [Phone Number]<br />
            <strong>Email:</strong> <a href="mailto:info@yourdomain.org">info@yourdomain.org</a>
          </p>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Send Us a Message</h3>
          <p class="muted">
            For general questions, please email us or use the form below.
            (You can connect this form to an email or database on your server.)
          </p>
          <form style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            <div style="margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
              <label for="name" style="display:block;font-size:0.9rem;">Name</label>
              <input id="name" type="text" style="width:100%;padding:0.45rem;border-radius:4px;border:1px solid #c5d1e0;" />
            </div>
            <div style="margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
              <label for="email" style="display:block;font-size:0.9rem;">Email</label>
              <input id="email" type="email" style="width:100%;padding:0.45rem;border-radius:4px;border:1px solid #c5d1e0;" />
            </div>
            <div style="margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
              <label for="message" style="display:block;font-size:0.9rem;">Message</label>
              <textarea id="message" rows="4" style="width:100%;padding:0.45rem;border-radius:4px;border:1px solid #c5d1e0;"></textarea>
            </div>
            <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Submit</button>
          </form>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>
</main>

<footer>
  <div class="footer-inner">
    <div>
      © <span id="year"></span> [Institute Name]. All rights reserved.
    </div>
    <div class="footer-links">
      <a href="#top">Back to top</a>
      <span>•</span>
      <a href="#support">Support Us</a>
      <span>•</span>
      <a href="#contact">Contact</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</footer>

<script>
  // Set current year in footer
  document.getElementById('year').textContent = new Date().getFullYear();
</script>

</body>
</html>

5. Next steps

If you tell me:

  • Your institute name and mission
  • Any color/logo preferences
  • Whether you need donation integration or legal text (e.g., 501(c)(3) number)

I can:

  • Customize this template specifically for you
  • Write more polished copy for each section
  • Suggest how to host it (e.g., GitHub Pages or a simple shared hosting account) and, if you want, give step-by-step instructions for deployment.

Your institute name and mission:

Any color/logo preferences
Whether you need donation integration or legal text (e.g., 501(c)(3) number

I can absolutely build this out for you, but I’ll need those details from you first so it’s not all placeholders.

Please fill in what you can (you can keep it brief; I’ll polish the wording):

  1. Institute name and location

    • Full name:
    • Short version / acronym (if any):
    • City, State/Region, Country:

  2. Mission (1–3 sentences, in your own words)
    Example you can edit:
    “We conduct innovative cancer research and provide high-quality education and training to improve outcomes for people affected by cancer.”

  3. Primary audiences (pick all that apply)

    • Patients / families
    • Researchers
    • Clinicians / hospitals
    • Students / trainees
    • Donors / foundations
    • General public / community

  4. Colors and branding

    • Main color (e.g., “navy blue”, hex code if you know it):
    • Secondary color(s):
    • Do you already have a logo?
      • Yes (describe colors/shape so I can match it)
      • No (I’ll keep it simple text-based for now)

  5. Donation setup
    Do you want a working “Donate” button now?

    • Not yet (just “Contact us about giving”)
    • Yes, via:
      • PayPal
      • Stripe
      • Network for Good
      • Other (which?):
    • Donation link URL (if you already have it):

  6. Legal / nonprofit info

    • Are you a registered 501(c)(3) (or other nonprofit status in your country)?
      • Yes
      • In progress
      • No / not applicable
    • If yes, do you want the registration number shown in the footer / Support page?
      • If yes, please provide the exact text (e.g., “Registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit, EIN 12‑3456789”):

  7. Cancer focus (if any)

    • General oncology, or specific focus (e.g., breast cancer, pediatric, hematologic, lung, etc.):

  8. Contact info for the site

    • Public email address (e.g., info@…):
    • Phone (if you want it listed):
    • Physical address (or city only, if you prefer):

Once you send that:

  • I’ll rewrite all the site text to match your exact mission, audience, and programs.
  • I’ll update the HTML/CSS template with:
    • Your institute name
    • Your colors
    • Correct contact and donation information
    • Appropriate nonprofit legal text in the Support and footer sections.

You’ll get a ready-to-use index.html file you can upload to your hosting or GitHub Pages.To customize the site for you, I need you to fill in some details. You can reply by copying the questions below and typing your answers after each one (short answers are fine; I’ll polish and integrate everything).

  1. Institute name and location

    • Full name:
    • Short version / acronym (if any):
    • City, State/Region, Country:

  2. Mission (1–3 sentences, in your own words)
    Example (you can edit this instead of writing from scratch):
    “We conduct innovative cancer research and provide high-quality education and training to improve outcomes for people affected by cancer.”

  3. Primary audiences (check all that apply)

    • Patients / families
    • Researchers
    • Clinicians / hospitals
    • Students / trainees
    • Donors / foundations
    • General public / community

  4. Colors and branding

    • Main color (e.g., “navy blue” or a hex code like #003366):
    • Secondary color(s):
    • Do you already have a logo? If yes, briefly describe its colors/style so the site can match it. If no, I’ll use simple text branding.

  5. Donation setup

    • Do you want a working “Donate” button right now, or just “Contact us about giving”?
    • If you already accept online donations, what platform and link do you use? (e.g., PayPal link, Stripe page, Network for Good, etc.)

  6. Legal / nonprofit info

    • Are you a registered 501(c)(3) (or equivalent in your country)? (Yes / In progress / No)
    • If yes and you want it on the site, please provide the exact text, such as:
      “Registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, EIN 12‑3456789.”

  7. Cancer focus

    • General oncology, or specific focus (e.g., breast cancer, pediatric cancer, hematologic malignancies, lung cancer, etc.):

  8. Contact info for the website (public-facing)

    • Email address:
    • Phone number (if you want it listed):
    • Physical address (full address, or just city/region if you prefer privacy):

Once you send these:

  • I’ll rewrite all the page content (Home, About, Research, Education, Support, News, Contact) specifically for your institute.
  • I’ll update the HTML/CSS template with your name, colors, contact and donation info, and nonprofit legal text, and give you a ready-to-upload index.html file.

I can absolutely build this out for you, but I’ll need those details from you first so it’s not all placeholders.

Please fill in what you can (you can keep it brief; I’ll polish the wording):
Full name: Institute 53: Advancing p53 Cancer Therapeutics
Short version / acronym (if any): Institute 53
City, State/Region, Country: Richmond, Virginia, USA
Mission (1–3 sentences, in your own words): Our mission is to reduce the global burden of cancer by accelerating safe, effective tumor-specific therapies that restore or leverage the p53 pathway and by delivering rigorous, accessible education to empower scientists to translate these advances into efficient therapy worldwide.
Primary audiences (pick all that apply)
Patients / families
Researchers
Clinicians / hospitals
Students / trainees
Donors / foundations
General public / community
Colors and branding
Main color: Pearl Blue (e.g., “navy blue”, hex code if you know it):
Secondary color(s): Dark Grass Green
Do you already have a logo? Yes
Yes (describe colors/shape so I can match it) attached as a PDF document
No (I’ll keep it simple text-based for now)
Donation setup
Do you want a working “Donate” button now?
Not yet (just “Contact us about giving”)
Yes, via:
PayPal
Stripe
Network for Good
Other (which?):
Donation link URL (if you already have it):
Legal / nonprofit info
Are you a registered 501(c)(3) (or other nonprofit status in your country)?
Yes
If yes, do you want the registration number shown in the footer / Support page?
“Registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit, EIN 41-3612025
General oncology, specifically any cany cancer with mutations in the p53 gene, which is more than 50% of human cancers:
Contact info for the site
Public email addres:s info@institute53.org
Phone (if you want it listed): 804-651-1141
Physical address (or city only, if you prefer): Richmond, Virginia
53 logo feathered arrow.pdf

Below is a customized, ready‑to-use one‑page site for Institute 53: Advancing p53 Cancer Therapeutics, with your mission, audiences, branding, and nonprofit info integrated.

You can:

  1. Copy everything below into a file named index.html.
  2. Open it in a browser to review.
  3. Upload to your hosting (or GitHub Pages/Netlify) when ready.

You mentioned a logo PDF; I’ll reference it as a placeholder image—you or your web person can replace the logo.png path with the actual logo file you export from the PDF.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <title>Institute 53: Advancing p53 Cancer Therapeutics</title>
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <meta name="description" content="Institute 53 is a nonprofit cancer research and teaching institute in Richmond, Virginia, focused on advancing safe, effective p53-targeted cancer therapies and training scientists worldwide.">

  <style>
    :root {
      /* Approximate colors based on your description */
      --primary: #4f7dac;        /* Pearl Blue (approx.) */
      --primary-light: #6f97c2;
      --accent: #2e7d32;         /* Dark Grass Green (approx.) */
      --bg: #f7f9fc;
      --text: #222;
      --muted: #555;
      --max-width: 1100px;
      --radius: 6px;
    }

    * {
      box-sizing: border-box;
      margin: 0;
      padding: 0;
    }

    body {
      font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif;
      color: var(--text);
      background: white;
      line-height: 1.6;
    }

    a {
      color: var(--primary);
      text-decoration: none;
    }

    a:hover {
      text-decoration: underline;
    }

    header {
      background: white;
      border-bottom: 1px solid #dde3ec;
      position: sticky;
      top: 0;
      z-index: 100;
    }

    .nav {
      max-width: var(--max-width);
      margin: 0 auto;
      padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: space-between;
      gap: 1rem;
    }

    .logo-wrap {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 0.65rem;
    }

    .logo-img {
      width: 40px;
      height: 40px;
      border-radius: 50%;
      object-fit: contain;
      background: #e5edf6;
    }

    .logo-text {
      font-weight: 700;
      color: var(--primary);
      font-size: 1.05rem;
      line-height: 1.2;
    }

    .logo-text span {
      display: block;
      font-weight: 400;
      color: var(--muted);
      font-size: 0.8rem;
    }

    nav ul {
      list-style: none;
      display: flex;
      gap: 1.25rem;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
    }

    nav a {
      font-weight: 500;
    }

    .hero {
      background: radial-gradient(circle at top left, #e3f2fd, #ffffff);
      border-bottom: 1px solid #dde3ec;
    }

    .hero-inner {
      max-width: var(--max-width);
      margin: 0 auto;
      padding: 3rem 1rem 3.5rem;
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1.5fr) minmax(0, 1.2fr);
      gap: 2rem;
      align-items: center;
    }

    .hero h1 {
      font-size: clamp(2rem, 3vw + 1rem, 3rem);
      margin-bottom: 1rem;
      color: #10375c;
    }

    .hero p {
      margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
      color: var(--muted);
      font-size: 1.02rem;
    }

    .hero-highlights {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 0.75rem 1.5rem;
      margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      color: var(--muted);
    }

    .hero-highlights span {
      display: inline-flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 0.35rem;
    }

    .dot {
      width: 6px;
      height: 6px;
      border-radius: 50%;
      background: var(--accent);
    }

    .button-row {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 0.75rem;
    }

    .btn {
      display: inline-block;
      padding: 0.65rem 1.25rem;
      border-radius: 999px;
      border: 1px solid transparent;
      font-weight: 600;
      font-size: 0.95rem;
      cursor: pointer;
      text-align: center;
    }

    .btn-primary {
      background: var(--primary);
      color: white;
      border-color: var(--primary);
    }

    .btn-primary:hover {
      background: var(--primary-light);
      text-decoration: none;
    }

    .btn-outline {
      background: white;
      color: var(--primary);
      border-color: var(--primary);
    }

    .btn-outline:hover {
      background: #e3f2fd;
      text-decoration: none;
    }

    .hero-panel {
      background: white;
      border-radius: var(--radius);
      padding: 1.5rem;
      box-shadow: 0 12px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
      border: 1px solid #e3e8f0;
    }

    .hero-panel h2 {
      font-size: 1.1rem;
      margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
    }

    .hero-panel ul {
      list-style: none;
      font-size: 0.92rem;
      color: var(--muted);
    }

    .hero-panel li {
      margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
      padding-left: 1.1rem;
      position: relative;
    }

    .hero-panel li::before {
      content: "•";
      position: absolute;
      left: 0;
      color: var(--accent);
    }

    main {
      padding: 2.5rem 0 3rem;
    }

    section {
      padding: 2.5rem 1rem;
    }

    .section-inner {
      max-width: var(--max-width);
      margin: 0 auto;
    }

    h2.section-title {
      font-size: 1.6rem;
      margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
      color: #10375c;
    }

    .two-column {
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1.3fr) minmax(0, 1fr);
      gap: 2rem;
      align-items: flex-start;
    }

    .card-grid {
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(230px, 1fr));
      gap: 1.25rem;
      margin-top: 1rem;
    }

    .card {
      background: white;
      border-radius: var(--radius);
      padding: 1.25rem;
      border: 1px solid #e3e8f0;
    }

    .card h3 {
      font-size: 1.05rem;
      margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
    }

    .muted {
      color: var(--muted);
      font-size: 0.95rem;
    }

    .list {
      list-style: disc;
      padding-left: 1.3rem;
    }

    .list li + li {
      margin-top: 0.35rem;
    }

    .pill {
      display: inline-block;
      padding: 0.25rem 0.7rem;
      border-radius: 999px;
      background: #e3f2fd;
      color: var(--primary);
      font-size: 0.8rem;
      font-weight: 600;
      letter-spacing: 0.03em;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
    }

    .highlight-box {
      border-left: 3px solid var(--accent);
      padding-left: 0.9rem;
      margin: 0.75rem 0 0.25rem;
    }

    .split {
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(260px, 1fr));
      gap: 1.5rem;
      margin-top: 0.5rem;
    }

    .badge-list {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 0.4rem;
      margin-top: 0.5rem;
    }

    .badge {
      padding: 0.2rem 0.6rem;
      border-radius: 999px;
      border: 1px solid #c5d1e0;
      font-size: 0.8rem;
      color: var(--muted);
      background: #f5f7fb;
    }

    footer {
      border-top: 1px solid #dde3ec;
      background: #f6f8fc;
      padding: 1.5rem 1rem;
      font-size: 0.9rem;
      color: var(--muted);
    }

    .footer-inner {
      max-width: var(--max-width);
      margin: 0 auto;
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: space-between;
      gap: 0.75rem;
    }

    .footer-links {
      display: flex;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 0.75rem;
    }

    /* Responsive */
    @media (max-width: 850px) {
      .hero-inner,
      .two-column {
        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
      }

      nav ul {
        display: none; /* Simple: hide desktop menu on small screens */
      }

      .hero-inner {
        padding-top: 2.25rem;
      }

      header {
        position: static;
      }
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>

<header>
  <div class="nav">
    <div class="logo-wrap">
      <!-- Replace src with your exported logo file path -->
      <img src="logo.png" alt="Institute 53 logo" class="logo-img" />
      <div class="logo-text">
        Institute 53
        <span>Advancing p53 Cancer Therapeutics</span>
      </div>
    </div>
    <nav aria-label="Main navigation">
      <ul>
        <li><a href="#about">About</a></li>
        <li><a href="#research">Research</a></li>
        <li><a href="#education">Education</a></li>
        <li><a href="#support">Support</a></li>
        <li><a href="#news">News & Events</a></li>
        <li><a href="#contact">Contact</a></li>
      </ul>
    </nav>
  </div>
</header>

<section class="hero" id="top">
  <div class="hero-inner">
    <div>
      <h1>Advancing p53 Cancer Therapeutics for Patients Worldwide</h1>
      <p>
        Institute 53 is a nonprofit research and teaching institute in Richmond, Virginia,
        dedicated to reducing the global burden of cancer by accelerating safe, effective
        tumor‑specific therapies that restore or leverage the p53 pathway, and by delivering
        rigorous, accessible education to empower scientists to translate these advances
        into efficient therapy worldwide.
      </p>
      <div class="hero-highlights">
        <span><span class="dot"></span> Focus on p53‑mutant cancers</span>
        <span><span class="dot"></span> Independent nonprofit institute</span>
        <span><span class="dot"></span> Global education & training</span>
        <span><span class="dot"></span> Bench‑to‑bedside collaborations</span>
      </div>
      <div class="button-row">
        <a href="#about" class="btn btn-primary">Learn More</a>
        <a href="#support" class="btn btn-outline">Contact Us About Giving</a>
      </div>
    </div>
    <aside class="hero-panel" aria-label="Key focus areas">
      <h2>Our Focus Areas</h2>
      <ul>
        <li>Therapies targeting cancers with p53 mutations (&gt;50% of human cancers).</li>
        <li>Strategies that restore, mimic, or harness p53 tumor suppressor function.</li>
        <li>Preclinical and translational research to enable safe, tumor‑specific treatment.</li>
        <li>Education programs that equip scientists and clinicians worldwide.</li>
      </ul>
    </aside>
  </div>
</section>

<main>
  <!-- About -->
  <section id="about">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">About Institute 53</h2>
      <div class="two-column">
        <div>
          <span class="pill">Our Mission</span>
          <p>
            Our mission is to reduce the global burden of cancer by accelerating safe,
            effective tumor‑specific therapies that restore or leverage the p53 pathway,
            and by delivering rigorous, accessible education to empower scientists to
            translate these advances into efficient therapy worldwide.
          </p>
          <div class="highlight-box">
            <strong>Vision:</strong> A future in which people everywhere benefit from
            precise, p53‑informed cancer therapies that are both effective and widely accessible.
          </div>
          <h3 style="margin-top:1.25rem;">Our Values</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li><strong>Scientific rigor</strong> – Designing and sharing reproducible, mechanistically grounded research.</li>
            <li><strong>Patient‑centered impact</strong> – Focusing on therapies that can truly improve patients’ lives.</li>
            <li><strong>Collaboration</strong> – Partnering with labs, clinicians, and institutions across disciplines and borders.</li>
            <li><strong>Education</strong> – Equipping current and future scientists to accelerate translation of p53 biology into therapy.</li>
            <li><strong>Equity</strong> – Working toward global access to emerging p53‑targeted treatments and knowledge.</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Who We Serve</h3>
          <p class="muted">
            Institute 53 sits at the intersection of cancer biology, translational research,
            and education. Our work is relevant to:
          </p>
          <div class="badge-list" style="margin-top:0.5rem;">
            <span class="badge">Patients & Families</span>
            <span class="badge">Researchers</span>
            <span class="badge">Clinicians & Hospitals</span>
            <span class="badge">Students & Trainees</span>
            <span class="badge">Donors & Foundations</span>
            <span class="badge">General Public</span>
          </div>
          <div class="card" style="margin-top:1rem;">
            <h3>Our Organization</h3>
            <p class="muted">
              Institute 53: Advancing p53 Cancer Therapeutics is an independent,
              registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit based in Richmond, Virginia, USA.
            </p>
            <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.4rem;">
              We collaborate with academic centers, hospitals, and research groups
              to move p53‑focused discoveries toward clinical benefit.
            </p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Research -->
  <section id="research" style="background: var(--bg);">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">Research</h2>
      <div class="two-column">
        <div>
          <span class="pill">Scientific Programs</span>
          <p>
            We focus on general oncology with a unifying theme: cancers driven by
            alterations in the p53 pathway. More than half of all human cancers
            harbor p53 mutations or pathway disruption. By centering our work on
            this critical tumor suppressor, we aim to unlock therapies that can
            benefit millions of patients worldwide.
          </p>
          <div class="card-grid">
            <div class="card">
              <h3>p53 Pathway Restoration</h3>
              <p class="muted">
                Exploring strategies to restore or reactivate p53 function in
                tumor cells, including small molecules, biologics, and gene‑based
                approaches.
              </p>
            </div>
            <div class="card">
              <h3>p53‑Directed Vulnerabilities</h3>
              <p class="muted">
                Identifying synthetic lethal interactions and selective
                vulnerabilities in p53‑mutant cancers to enable tumor‑specific therapies.
              </p>
            </div>
            <div class="card">
              <h3>Translational & Preclinical Models</h3>
              <p class="muted">
                Developing and applying laboratory models that more accurately
                reflect human p53‑mutant tumors to de‑risk clinical translation.
              </p>
            </div>
            <div class="card">
              <h3>Global Impact & Access</h3>
              <p class="muted">
                Studying how p53‑targeted strategies can be implemented in
                diverse health systems and resource settings to broaden access.
              </p>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Selected Research Themes</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li>
              <strong>Mechanistic studies of p53 mutations</strong> – Clarifying how
              specific variants alter tumor behavior and therapeutic response.
            </li>
            <li>
              <strong>Combination strategies</strong> – Pairing p53‑directed agents
              with immunotherapies, DNA‑damaging agents, or targeted drugs.
            </li>
            <li>
              <strong>Biomarkers & patient selection</strong> – Defining who is
              most likely to benefit from emerging p53‑focused treatments.
            </li>
          </ul>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            We welcome collaboration inquiries from investigators and institutions
            working on p53 biology, drug discovery, clinical trials, or implementation
            science.
          </p>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.5rem;">
            To learn more about current projects or potential partnerships,
            please <a href="#contact">contact us</a>.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Education -->
  <section id="education">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">Education & Training</h2>
      <div class="split">
        <div>
          <span class="pill">Training the Next Generation</span>
          <p>
            Institute 53 is committed to rigorous, accessible education that helps
            scientists, clinicians, and trainees translate p53 science into real‑world
            cancer therapeutics.
          </p>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li>
              <strong>Research‑based Training</strong> – Opportunities for students
              and early‑career scientists to engage with p53‑focused projects.
            </li>
            <li>
              <strong>Workshops & Short Courses</strong> – Focused programs on p53
              biology, experimental design, and translational strategies.
            </li>
            <li>
              <strong>Seminars & Case Discussions</strong> – Sessions that connect
              laboratory discoveries to clinical decision‑making.
            </li>
            <li>
              <strong>Open Educational Resources</strong> – Accessible materials
              designed to support learners worldwide.
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Audiences We Support</h3>
          <p class="muted">
            Our educational efforts reach:
          </p>
          <div class="badge-list">
            <span class="badge">Students & Trainees</span>
            <span class="badge">Academic & Industry Scientists</span>
            <span class="badge">Clinicians & Hospitals</span>
            <span class="badge">Patient Advocates</span>
            <span class="badge">Global Partners</span>
          </div>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            As our programs develop, we will share specific offerings, application
            details, and schedules here.
          </p>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.4rem;">
            For current opportunities or to explore co‑developed educational
            initiatives, please <a href="#contact">get in touch</a>.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Support -->
  <section id="support" style="background: var(--bg);">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">Support Our Work</h2>
      <div class="two-column">
        <div>
          <span class="pill">Nonprofit Impact</span>
          <p>
            As a nonprofit institute, Institute 53 relies on philanthropic support
            to accelerate p53‑focused research and to expand our educational reach.
          </p>
          <p class="muted">
            Your support helps us:
          </p>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li>Advance high‑impact projects in p53‑mutant cancers.</li>
            <li>Develop safer, more tumor‑specific therapeutic strategies.</li>
            <li>Provide training and mentorship for scientists and clinicians.</li>
            <li>Create educational resources accessible to a global audience.</li>
          </ul>
          <div class="button-row" style="margin-top:1rem;">
            <!-- No live donation link yet; invite conversation instead -->
            <a href="#contact" class="btn btn-primary">Contact Us About Giving</a>
          </div>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.6rem;font-size:0.85rem;">
            Institute 53: Advancing p53 Cancer Therapeutics is a registered
            501(c)(3) nonprofit organization (EIN 41-3612025). Contributions may
            be tax‑deductible as allowed by law. Please consult your tax advisor.
          </p>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Other Ways to Get Involved</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li><strong>Research partnerships</strong> – Collaborate on p53 biology, preclinical models, or translational studies.</li>
            <li><strong>Educational collaborations</strong> – Co‑develop courses, workshops, or seminars focused on p53 and cancer therapeutics.</li>
            <li><strong>Advisory roles</strong> – Share expertise in oncology, drug development, regulation, or global health.</li>
            <li><strong>Spread the word</strong> – Introduce Institute 53 to potential partners, trainees, or supporters.</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- News & Events -->
  <section id="news">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">News & Events</h2>
      <div class="split">
        <div>
          <h3>Latest News</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li>
              <strong>[Date]</strong> – Institute 53 launches its mission to advance
              p53‑targeted cancer therapeutics from Richmond, Virginia.
            </li>
            <li>
              <strong>[Date]</strong> – Early collaborations established with
              partners exploring p53‑mutant tumor biology and therapy.
            </li>
          </ul>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.5rem;">
            As our work grows, we will post publications, grants, and key milestones here.
          </p>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Upcoming Events</h3>
          <ul class="list muted">
            <li>
              <strong>[Event Name]</strong> – [Date]. Introductory virtual session
              on Institute 53’s focus and opportunities for collaboration.
            </li>
            <li>
              <strong>[Seminar/Workshop]</strong> – [Date]. Educational event on
              p53‑mutant cancers and translational challenges.
            </li>
          </ul>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.5rem;">
            To receive updates about future events and educational offerings,
            please <a href="#contact">contact us</a>.
          </p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- Contact -->
  <section id="contact" style="background: var(--bg);">
    <div class="section-inner">
      <h2 class="section-title">Contact</h2>
      <div class="split">
        <div>
          <p>
            We welcome inquiries from prospective collaborators, trainees,
            donors, and members of the public who share our interest in p53‑mutant
            cancers and tumor‑specific therapies.
          </p>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            <strong>Location:</strong><br />
            Richmond, Virginia, USA
          </p>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.5rem;">
            <strong>Phone:</strong> <a href="tel:+18046511141">804-651-1141</a><br />
            <strong>Email:</strong> <a href="mailto:info@institute53.org">info@institute53.org</a>
          </p>
          <p class="muted" style="margin-top:0.5rem;font-size:0.9rem;">
            For media or speaking requests, collaboration proposals, or questions
            about support, please reach out via email or phone.
          </p>
        </div>
        <div>
          <h3>Send Us a Message</h3>
          <p class="muted">
            This simple form can be wired to your email or a database by your
            web administrator. Until then, messages will not be sent automatically.
          </p>
          <form style="margin-top:0.75rem;">
            <div style="margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
              <label for="name" style="display:block;font-size:0.9rem;">Name</label>
              <input id="name" type="text" style="width:100%;padding:0.45rem;border-radius:4px;border:1px solid #c5d1e0;" />
            </div>
            <div style="margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
              <label for="email" style="display:block;font-size:0.9rem;">Email</label>
              <input id="email" type="email" style="width:100%;padding:0.45rem;border-radius:4px;border:1px solid #c5d1e0;" />
            </div>
            <div style="margin-bottom:0.5rem;">
              <label for="message" style="display:block;font-size:0.9rem;">Message</label>
              <textarea id="message" rows="4" style="width:100%;padding:0.45rem;border-radius:4px;border:1px solid #c5d1e0;"></textarea>
            </div>
            <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Submit (not yet active)</button>
          </form>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>
</main>

<footer>
  <div class="footer-inner">
    <div>
      © <span id="year"></span> Institute 53: Advancing p53 Cancer Therapeutics. All rights reserved.
    </div>
    <div class="footer-links">
      <span>Registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit, EIN 41-3612025</span>
      <span>•</span>
      <a href="#top">Back to top</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</footer>

<script>
  // Set current year in footer
  document.getElementById('year').textContent = new Date().getFullYear();
</script>

</body>
</html>

If you’d like, I can next:

  • Adjust the colors to match exact hex values from your logo (once you share them or an image export).
  • Add a real PayPal/Stripe donate button when you’re ready.
  • Give step‑by‑step instructions for putting this live on GitHub Pages or another host.

Give step‑by‑step instructions for putting this live on GitHub Pages or another host.

Deploying Your Institute 53 Website – Step-by-Step

Here are the easiest options, from simplest to most flexible:

Option 1: GitHub Pages (Free, Recommended for Nonprofits)

Time: 5–10 minutes | Cost: $0 | Custom domain later: Yes

Step 1: Create a GitHub Account

  • Go to github.com and sign up (free).
  • Use an email like web@institute53.org or your personal one.

Step 2: Create a New Repository

  1. Click the "+" (top right) → "New repository".
  2. Repository name: institute53 (or institute53-site).
  3. Public: Yes (required for free GitHub Pages).
  4. Check "Add a README file".
  5. Click "Create repository".

Step 3: Upload Your Website

  1. In your new repo, click "Add file" → "Upload files".
  2. Drag the index.html file (from my code above) into the upload area.
  3. Optional: Drag your logo PNG/JPG into the same area.
  4. Scroll down, add commit message: "Launch Institute 53 website".
  5. Click "Commit changes".

Step 4: Enable GitHub Pages

  1. Click "Settings" tab (top of repo).
  2. Scroll to "Pages" (left sidebar).
  3. Under "Source":
    • Branch: main (or master)
    • Folder: / (root)
  4. Click "Save".

Step 5: Access Your Live Site

  • Wait 1–3 minutes.
  • Go to Settings → Pages again.
  • Find your URL: https://yourusername.github.io/institute53
  • Your live site: https://institute53.org (if you add custom domain later).

✅ Done! Share the URL immediately.

Option 2: Netlify (Even Easier Drag‑and‑Drop)

Time: 2 minutes | Cost: $0 | Custom domain: Yes

  1. Go to netlify.com → "Sign up" (free).
  2. "Deploy manually" → Drag your index.html (and logo) folder onto the dashed area.
  3. Instant URL: https://amazing-name-123.netlify.app
  4. Rename site (Sites → your site → Site settings → Site details → Site name).

✅ Live in seconds. Upgrade later for custom domain.

Option 3: Shared Hosting (Namecheap, GoDaddy, etc.)

If you already have hosting/domain:

  1. Upload files:
    • File Manager (cPanel) → public_html folder.
    • Upload index.html and logo.
  2. Visit: yourdomain.com (replace index.html with your domain).
  3. Set homepage: Rename to index.html if needed.

Updating Your Site Later

GitHub Pages (easiest):

  1. Go to repo → Edit index.html → Change text/logo → Commit changes.
  2. Live in 1–2 minutes.

Pro tip: Edit directly in GitHub's web editor—no software needed.

Custom Domain (e.g., institute53.org)

After choosing hosting:

  1. GitHub Pages:

    • Settings → Pages → "Custom domain" → Enter institute53.org.
    • Add CNAME file to repo root with just institute53.org.

  2. DNS settings (at your domain registrar):

    A record → 185.199.108.153
A record → 185.199.109.153  
A record → 185.199.110.153
A record → 185.199.111.153
CNAME → www → yourusername.github.io

Test: Visit institute53.org after 1–48 hours.

Quick Checklist

✅ GitHub account
✅ Repo created (`institute53`)
✅ index.html uploaded  
✅ GitHub Pages enabled
✅ Site URL works: https://yourusername.github.io/institute53
✅ Logo uploaded (optional)
✅ Custom domain (optional, later)

Need help? Reply with your GitHub username or any error, and I'll troubleshoot.

Your p53 cancer therapeutics site will be

Give step‑by‑step instructions for putting this live on GitHub Pages or another host.

Deploy Institute 53 Site to GitHub Pages (5–10 Minutes, Free)

Step 1: GitHub Account

  • Go to github.com → Sign up (free).
  • Use info@institute53.org or a personal email.

Step 2: Create Repository

  1. Click "+" (top right) → "New repository".
  2. Repository name: institute53 (exact).
  3. Public: ✓ (required for free Pages).
  4. Add README: ✓.
  5. "Create repository".

Step 3: Upload Files

  1. In repo → "Add file" → "Upload files".
  2. Drag index.html (from my code) + logo.png (export from PDF).
  3. Commit message: "Launch Institute 53 website".
  4. "Commit changes".

Step 4: Enable GitHub Pages

  1. "Settings" tab.
  2. Left sidebar → "Pages".
  3. Source: Deploy from a branch.
  4. Branch: main, Folder: / (root).
  5. "Save".

Step 5: Live Site

  • Wait 1–3 minutes.
  • Pages section shows URL: https://yourusername.github.io/institute53.
  • Test: Visit URL (refresh if needed).

✅ Site live! Share immediately.

Update Later (2 Minutes)

  1. Repo → Edit index.html → Change text/logo.
  2. "Commit changes".
  3. Live in 1 minute.

Custom Domain (institute53.org)

After site works:

  1. GitHub: Settings → Pages → Custom domain → institute53.org → Save.
  2. Your domain registrar (Namecheap/GoDaddy):
    A → 185.199.108.153
A → 185.199.109.153  
A → 185.199.110.153
A → 185.199.111.153
CNAME www → yourusername.github.io
  3. Wait 1–48 hours → institute53.org works.

Alternative: Netlify (2 Minutes Drag‑Drop)

  1. netlify.com → Sign up.
  2. "Deploy manually" → Drag index.html + logo folder.
  3. Live: random-name.netlify.app.
  4. Rename → Custom domain later.

Troubleshooting

Issue Fix
404 error Wait 3 min, refresh, check branch=main
Logo missing Export PDF to PNG, re-upload as logo.png
Mobile menu Hidden (desktop first); add hamburger later
Colors off Reply with logo hex codes, I'll adjust CSS

Your p53 site will be live at https://yourusername.github.io/institute53 shortly after Step 5.

Reply with your GitHub username or any error for help235

Web Search
AI can make mistakes. Double-check important information.
